folar-de-olhao-teleculinaria.name: Olhão Folar

ingredients.item: 1  saqueta de fermento de padeiro seco

instructions: In a bowl, dissolve the yeast well into warm milk. Then add the eggs, orange juice, butter, fennel and flour. Knead well until a very homogeneous mass. If necessary, sprinkle with a little more flour.
Place the dough in a bowl, cover with a damp cloth and let it leave till it gets twice the volume.

At that time, knead the dough again and spread it on the bench sprinkled with flour. Garnish it with soft butter and sprinkle it with yellow sugar and cinnamon. Cut wide strips and wrap them around each other, as you see in the photo. Place it in a shape (22 cm in diameter), previously greased with butter, and leave it leave for another 30 minutes.
After the time indicated, brush the folar with the mixture of yolk with milk. Take it in the oven, preheated to 180oC, for about 35 minutes. Take it off, peel it off and let it cool. Serve sliced.

octopus-stew-in-the-oven-argiro.name: Octopus Stew In The Oven

ingredients.item: 5 medium onions cut into thick slices
6 medium potatoes cut into thick pieces
5 medium onions cut into thick slices
6 medium potatoes cut into thick pieces

instructions: Clean the octopus first and then marinate. If we use frozen octopus, let it thaw properly into the refrigerator maintenance.
Cut the octopus into tentacles.
Add the olive oil and vinegar.
Strain the orange.
Add the peel from the orange, spices and herbs.
Mix, cover and set aside to marinate.
In a deep pan, add the olive oil, heat it over medium heat.
Cut the onions into thick slices and add them to the hot oil.
Sauté for 7-8 minutes, stringing until the onion becomes transparent and slow starts caramelizing.
Break the garlic and add them to the pan, sauté for 1 minute, stirring.
Add the paste and rub it on the base of the pan.
Bake for 1 minute and add the vinegar and allow the ingredients to dry.
Finally, add the wine and turn off the stove.
Clean the potatoes and cut them into thick slices.
In a deep baking pan add the potatoes in a layer, add the saved onions and all the liquids in the pan.
Add the tomatoes cut into thick pieces.
Finally, enter the marinated octopus tentacles into the materials.
Add any liquid left over from the marinade.
Add salt and pepper and mix the ingredients. Attention! Salt by measure because octopus can already be salty.
Cover the baking pan with non-stick paper and seal around the baking pan with aluminum foil.
Alternatively you can use a pot with lid.

Bake in a well preheated oven for 1 hour at 170 degrees in air. Uncover the baking pan and continue baking for about 35 more minutes, until the octopus stew stays almost with its oil, softens and melts the potatoes by sucking all the octopus zooms.
Serve the octopus stew in the oven with melted potatoes and season salad.

old-clothes-teleculinaria.name: Old Clothes

ingredients.item: Q.b.  sobras de couve portuguesa cozida

instructions: Cook the eggs in salted water for 20 minutes, remove them and cool them in cold water.
Clean the cod of skin and pimples and chip it. Cut the potatoes, cabbage and carrots into pieces. Peel the garlic teeth and pick them finely.

Bring a pot to the fire with the olive oil and the laurel. When it warms, add the garlic, stir and saute. Add the cod, potatoes, kale and carrots, refine the salt, season with pepper and wrap well. Let it cook, until it heats well. Serve with the boiled eggs, cut into slices.

olive-oil-mashed-potatoes-with-spinach-a.name: Olive Oil-mashed Potatoes With Spinach And Basil
instructions: Cook potatoes in large pot of boiling salted water until tender, 15 to 20 minutes. Drain; return to pot. Stir over low heat until excess moisture evaporates, 1 to 2 minutes. Using potato masher, mash warm potatoes. Mix in 6 tablespoons oil and 6 tablespoons chicken broth and stir to blend. Season to taste with coarse salt and freshly ground black pepper.

Heat 1 tablespoon oil in heavy large skillet over medium-high heat. Add spinach and toss 1 minute. Add basil leaves and toss until wilted, 1 to 2 minutes longer. Transfer mixture to sieve set over bowl. Drain well.

Rewarm potatoes, adding more broth to moisten as needed. Stir in spinach mixture. Mound potatoes in bowl and serve.
Do Ahead: Potatoes and spinach mixture can be made 2 hours ahead. Let stand separately at room temperature.

one-pan-autumn-chicken-breasts-with-appl.name: One-pan Autumn Chicken Breasts With Apples

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

ingredients.prep: cored and thinly sliced (such as Autumn Glory®)

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil and butter in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Season both sides of the chicken breasts with salt and pepper and add to the hot skillet, skin-side down, leaving at least 1 inch between the breasts to ensure even cooking and browning. Cook until skin is browned, about 6 minutes. Turn and cook an additional 6 minutes until browned on both sides. Transfer chicken breasts to a plate.
Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C).

Add remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil to the skillet and stir in apples and onion. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 4 minutes. Season with sage, rosemary, thyme, salt, poultry seasoning, and pepper; stir to combine.

While apples are cooking, whisk together chicken broth, whipping cream, honey, and mustard in a small bowl. Pour broth mixture in with the seasoned apples and onions and gently scrape browned bits from the bottom using a wooden spoon. Slide chicken breasts back into the skillet, nestling them between onions and apples.

Transfer skillet to the preheated oven and cook until chicken is no longer pink at the bone and the juices run clear, about 30 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted near the bone should read 74°C (74 degrees C).

orange-and-carrot-compote-teleculinaria.name: Orange And Carrot Compote
instructions: With the help of a knife, cut the orange peel into strips and throw them in a pot. Squeeze the orange juice and lie in the pot as well as lemon juice. Add the water and bring it to the fire until boiling.
Then reduce the heat, cover and boil for about 1 hour until the shells are soft.
Add the grated carrot and sugar and stir it in a light until it is dissolved. Increase the heat and let it boil for another 5 to 10 minutes, so as to keep the consistency of jam.
Retire o tacho do lume e deixe arrefecer por cerca de 5 minutos. Distribua a compota por frascos esterilizados, feche-os e guarde-os em local seco e escuro.

4. Oil cake mold, pour mortar.

orange-caramelized-tarte-tatin-lezzet.name: Orange Caramelized Tarte Tatin
instructions: 1. For caramelizing process, take powder sugar and juice into a sauce pot.
2. Heat the sugar until you start boiling.
3. After boiling, boil 5 minutes for the darkening of the color and get from the stove.
4. thoroughly broadcast on the base of a cake mold with 20 cm.
5. Clean white parts on black.
6. Put the oranges you slice round and fill the fill with almonds.
7. Combine straws and open the size of the weighing mold.
8. Close the oranges with the ruby dough you open above.

orange-cranberry-scones.name: Orange-cranberry Scones
instructions: Rehydrate the cranberries by placing them in a small bowl and covering with boiling water. Let sit for at least 10 minutes, until plump, then drain and spread out on a paper towel to dry.

In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, granulated sugar, and baking powder. Use a pastry cutter (or your fingers) to incorporate the Private Selection® Salted French Butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Gently fold in the cranberries.
In a medium bowl or measuring cup, beat the eggs, then add the heavy cream, vanilla, and orange zest and whisk to combine.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and gently fold with a spatula to combine; do not overmix.

Place a sheet of parchment on your work surface and lightly dust with flour. Turn the dough out onto the floured parchment and pat into a circle about 7 inches in diameter. Lift the parchment paper onto a baking sheet and refrigerate the dough round uncovered for at least 20 minutes, up to 45 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 204°C (200°C)
Use a floured bench scraper to cut the dough round into 8 wedges. Separate the wedges and carefully space evenly on the baking sheet.
Make the egg wash: In a small bowl, whisk together the egg and cream. Brush each scone generously with the egg wash.
Bake the scones for about 20 minutes, until they are puffed and golden brown. Remove from the oven and let cool completely.

Make the orange drizzle: In a medium bowl, whisk together the powdered sugar, orange zest, and orange juice until smooth. Either transfer to a piping bag and snip off the corner or use a spoon to drizzle the glaze back and forth over the scones.
Enjoy!

oven-roasted-chicken-adobo.name: Oven-roasted Chicken Adobo

ingredients.item: Johnny’s seasoning salt or kosher salt
Cracked black pepper; plus more freshly ground
Garlic fried rice and winter greens laing

instructions: Preheat oven to 375°. Pat chicken thighs dry, then season very lightly all over with seasoning salt (or kosher salt and pepper). Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Arrange chicken, skin side down, in pan and cook until deeply browned underneath, 8–10 minutes. Turn chicken over and cook until lightly browned on the other side, about 3 minutes. Transfer to a roasting dish just large enough to fit chicken in a single layer and arrange skin side up; set aside. Pour out and discard all but 2 Tbsp. chicken fat from the pan.

Set skillet over medium heat. Add onion and garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until garlic is golden and onion is translucent, about 3 minutes. Add vinegar, scraping up any browned bits stuck to bottom of skillet with a wooden spoon, then add soy sauce, liquid seasoning, bay leaves, ½ tsp. cracked pepper, and ½ cup water. Bring to a simmer, then remove from heat and pour over reserved chicken in baking dish (chicken should be mostly covered with only skin left exposed).
Bake chicken, adding a splash or so of water if dish is looking dry at any point, until meat is almost falling off the bone, 60–75 minutes.
Divide rice among plates and nestle chicken thighs alongside, dividing evenly. Serve with laing.

oxtail-and-red-wine-stew.name: Oxtail And Red Wine Stew

ingredients.prep: white and pale green parts only, halved lengthwise, then sliced crosswise into  0.5" pieces
stems tied together with kitchen twine
grated Parmesan, and/or sliced jarred pepperoncini (for serving; optional)

instructions: Heat oil in a medium skillet over medium. As soon as oil is shimmering, add panko and cook, stirring often, until golden brown, about 3 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Remove from heat and mix in chives and thyme. Let cool.

Grate beet on the large holes of a box grater into a medium bowl. Grate about 1” of horseradish on the small holes of grater into the same bowl. Add vinegar and honey and season with salt and pepper; mix well. Let sit at room temperature at least 15 minutes before serving.
Do Ahead: Relish can be made 2 days ahead. Cover and chill.

Season oxtails very generously all over with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot over medium-high. Working in 2 batches, cook oxtail in a single layer, turning once, until browned on both sides, 8–10 minutes per batch. As oxtail pieces finish cooking, transfer to a large plate.

Reduce heat to medium and cook leeks, celery, and garlic, cut side down, in the same pot, stirring leeks and celery occasionally, until leeks are softened and browned in spots and garlic is golden brown, about 5 minutes. Add tomato paste and cook, stirring constantly and scraping bottom of pot, until vegetables are coated and paste is slightly darkened in color, about 3 minutes. Gradually add wine, stirring to release any bits stuck on bottom of pot, then add 6 cups water and thyme. Season generously with salt and pepper and bring to a simmer. Return oxtails to pot and bring stew back up to a simmer. Cover with a lid, leaving slightly askew so steam can escape and cook, adjusting heat to maintain a very gentle simmer and skimming foam from surface as needed, until meat is fork-tender, 3–3½ hours.

Uncover and remove pot from heat. Transfer oxtails to a cutting board and let cool 15–20 minutes. Pull meat from bones and tear into bite-size pieces; discard fat, cartilage, and bones. Add meat back to pot as you go. Let stew cool uncovered until no longer steaming. Cover pot and chill stew at least 12 hours (you can skip this step, but it will dramatically improve the flavor).

Uncover stew and spoon off half to three-quarters of fat on the surface; discard. Gently reheat stew until barely simmering. Pluck out and discard thyme and garlic heads (don't worry about any cloves that may have escaped into stew). Taste and season with more salt and pepper if needed. Ladle into bowls and serve with Herby Panko, Horseradish and Beet Relish, parsley, Parmesan, and/or pepperoncini as desired.
Do Ahead: Stew can be made 3 days ahead. Keep chilled.
